title: Encapsulated submersible motor unit with capacitor housing.
abstract: A submersible motor assembly to be submerged in a liquid body comprises a motor to drive a pump to facilitate pumping of a liquid from the liquid body. The submersible motor assembly includes a motor body to enclose the motor. Further, the submersible motor assembly includes a capacitor housing disposed outside of the motor body. The capacitor housing is also co-axial to the motor body. The submersible motor assembly includes a capacitor housed in the capacitor housing, which is coupled to the motor to facilitate starting of the motor.
claims:
Claims I/ We claim: 1. A submersible motor assembly to be submerged in a liquid body, the submersible motor assembly comprising: a motor to drive a pump to facilitate pumping of a liquid from the liquid body; a motor body to enclose the motor; a capacitor housing disposed outside of and being co-axial to the motor body; and a capacitor housed in the capacitor housing and coupled to the motor to facilitate starting of the motor. 2. The submersible motor assembly as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the capacitor housing is cup-shaped. 3. The submersible motor assembly as claimed in claim 2, wherein the capacitor housing comprises: a base; a flange; a side wall extending from the base to the flange; and a plurality of ribs circumferentially-spaced on the side wall. 4. The submersible motor assembly as claimed in claim 1 , comprising: a capacitor top plate disposed on the capacitor housing, wherein the capacitor housing comprises: a base on which the capacitor is to be placed; a flange displaced at a distance from the base to support the capacitor top plate; and a side wall extending from the base to the flange. 5. The submersible motor assembly as claimed in claim 4, wherein the capacitor comprises: a first lead wire connected to the motor; and 

 a second lead wire connected to the motor, wherein the capacitor housing comprises: a first housing slot through which the first lead wire passes for connecting to the motor; and a second housing slot that is diametrically opposite the first housing slot through which the second lead wire passes for connecting to the motor. 6. The submersible motor assembly as claimed in claim 5, wherein the capacitor housing comprises: a first housing member extending from the flange to the side wall, wherein the first housing member encloses a first flange opening on the flange and a first wall opening on the side wall to form the first housing slot; and a second housing member extending from the flange to the side wall, wherein the second housing member encloses a second flange opening on the flange and a second wall opening on the side wall to form the second housing slot. 7. The submersible motor assembly as claimed in claim 6, comprising: a capacitor top plate disposed on the capacitor housing, wherein, a first plate slot is formed on the capacitor top plate, wherein the first lead wire passes through the first plate slot to connect to the motor; and a second plate slot is formed on the capacitor top plate that is diametrically opposite the first plate slot, wherein the second lead wire passes through the second plate slot to connect to the motor. 8. The submersible motor assembly as claimed in claim 1 , comprising: 

 a lower housing coupled to the motor body and being co-axial to the motor body to house a motor shaft, wherein the capacitor housing is detachably coupled to the lower housing. 9. The submersible motor assembly as claimed in claim 8, comprising: a capacitor top plate disposed on an end of the capacitor housing to facilitate coupling of the capacitor housing with the lower housing; and a diaphragm disposed on the capacitor top plate to expand when a pressure inside the motor changes or when temperature of a cooling liquid inside the motor changes during operation of the motor. 10. The submersible motor assembly as claimed in claim 9, wherein: the capacitor top plate is cup-shaped, the diaphragm is cup-shaped, the diaphragm and the capacitor top plate are co-axial to the capacitor housing. 1 1. The submersible motor assembly as claimed in claim 9, wherein the capacitor top plate comprises: an inlet to facilitate entry of the liquid from the liquid body; and a duct extending from the inlet to supply liquid to the diaphragm through an opening formed on the capacitor top plate. 12. The submersible motor assembly as claimed in claim 9, comprising: a base cover coupled to the capacitor housing and enclosing the capacitor housing and the capacitor top plate, wherein the base cover is co-axial to the capacitor housing.
